goal: Tell Wency when a proposal/action stages for confirmation, via web-push (PWA Service Worker) as the MVP channel behind a vendor-agnostic adapter seam, with preferences + quiet hours; triggers are fire-and-forget (never break stagePendingAction); WhatsApp deferred (ADR-008).
why: Wency's meeting ask was 'tell me like a WhatsApp when an invoice is ready'. Web-push delivers that immediately with zero vendor onboarding, riding the Phase-1 PWA manifest; the adapter seam means the WhatsApp follow-on is one file, not a refactor.
success_criteria: When a destructive action stages (stagePendingAction), the signed-in operator with a registered subscription and a 'stage' opt-in gets an OS web-push notification on their installed PWA, outside quiet hours.
A notification delivery failure NEVER throws or fails the stagePendingAction insert; it is logged to notification_log and swallowed (fire-and-forget, test-proven).
The notify layer is a vendor-agnostic NotificationChannel adapter; web-push is one implementation; adding WhatsApp later is one new file with no change to triggers/routes.
notification_preferences + notification_log exist (migration 0019), RLS-on / service-role-only, principal-CHECK-constrained, every query scoped to the session principal.
The operator can enable push, toggle the stage event, and set wrap-midnight quiet hours from /settings; the iOS 'install first' caveat is shown.

WhatsApp/Telnyx is NOT built (deferred per ADR-008); only the adapter seam is left ready. Owned by Task 2 (test ! -f whatsapp.ts + grep telnyx/whatsapp under src/lib/notify = 0) and the whole-phase deferral contract.
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0 and all new tests pass — cross-cutting, enforced by every task Validation block plus the whole-phase compiles+builds Verification Contract.

tasks.title: Migration: notification_preferences + notification_log (0019)
acceptance_criteria: notification_preferences keyed by principal with channel, enabled_events (jsonb), quiet_hours_start/end (nullable time), push_subscription (nullable jsonb), timestamps; principal is unique.
notification_log with id, principal, channel, event, sent_at, error (nullable), metadata (jsonb); index on (principal, sent_at).
Both tables: principal text not null check (principal in ('Wency','Jeanette')), matching 0010_pending_actions.sql.
Both tables: RLS enabled with NO policies (service-role-only), matching the project pattern; justification stated in a SQL comment (no Supabase Auth, principal lives in a signed cookie).
tasks.action: Mirror 0010_pending_actions.sql header + structure. enabled_events default '["stage"]'. Quiet-hours wrap logic lives in code, not schema. 90-day audit enforced at query time (SQL comment, no DB cron).

tasks.title: Adapter seam + web-push channel + Service Worker
acceptance_criteria: adapter.ts exports a NotificationChannel interface (name + send(principal, message, subscription)) and a NotifyMessage type; it contains NO web-push specifics (the seam).

webpush.ts imports and implements the NotificationChannel seam; webPushChannel.send() never throws: WebPushError 410/404 -> { ok:false, expired:true }, other error -> { ok:false, error }, missing VAPID keys -> { ok:false } without calling sendNotification.
public/sw.js has push (showNotification) + notificationclick handlers.
web-push is in package.json dependencies; getVapidEnv() added to env.ts with all fields optional (app boots without keys).
WhatsApp/Telnyx is NOT built: no src/lib/notify/whatsapp.ts and no telnyx/whatsapp reference under src/lib/notify (ADR-008 defers it; only the adapter seam is ready).
tasks.action: npm install web-push + -D @types/web-push. adapter.ts is vendor-agnostic, imports Principal from @/lib/openrouter/client. webpush.ts imports NotificationChannel from the adapter and implements it; uses setVapidDetails + sendNotification, wrapped in try/catch. env getVapidEnv reads VAPID keys optionally (try/catch returning null). webpush.test.ts mocks web-push: 410 maps to expired, generic error does not throw, missing keys skips send.

tasks.title: Fire-and-forget trigger + preferences lib + API routes (subscribe + preferences)
acceptance_criteria: notifyOnStage(principal, action) gates on push_subscription present + 'stage' opt-in + not within quiet hours; otherwise sends nothing.
On send it builds a NotifyMessage from action.summary, calls webPushChannel.send, logs the outcome to notification_log; on expired (410/404) clears push_subscription.
stagePendingAction calls notifyOnStage AFTER the insert via void notifyOnStage(...).catch(()=>{}) and still returns the staged PendingAction unchanged; the hook can never throw into the insert path.
isWithinQuietHours handles wrap-midnight (start 22:00 / end 07:00 -> 23:30 and 06:00 quiet, 12:00 not).
triggers.ts does NOT import pendingActions (no cycle).
POST /api/notify/subscribe: principal via getPrincipal (401 if absent), Zod-validates a PushSubscriptionJSON, upserts push_subscription via savePreferences.
GET /api/notify/preferences: returns the principal's prefs via loadPreferences, creating a default row (enabled_events ['stage'], no quiet hours, channel webpush) if none.
POST /api/notify/preferences: Zod-validates { enabled_events?, quiet_hours_start?, quiet_hours_end? }, updates only provided fields via savePreferences; push subscription NOT settable here.
All handlers 401 without a session principal; runtime nodejs + dynamic force-dynamic; routes NOT added to the src/proxy.ts cron allowlist.
tasks.action: preferences.ts: loadPreferences/savePreferences(upsert)/clearExpiredSubscription scoped to principal; isWithinQuietHours with wrap. triggers.ts: load, gate, send, log, clear-on-expired; body in try/catch, never re-throws. pendingActions.ts: void notifyOnStage(...).catch after insert. Both /api/notify routes model on api/actions/route.ts, go through the preferences helpers, Zod-validate (400 on bad), NOT in proxy allowlist. Tests: no-throw, gating, clear-on-expired, 401, principal-scoping.

tasks.title: Settings UI: enable push + preferences + quiet hours
acceptance_criteria: /settings renders under the app shell with the maritime dark-ocean tokens (matches Emails/Finance/Tasks).
Enable-notifications control: registers /sw.js, requests permission, pushManager.subscribe with the VAPID public key (NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY), POSTs the subscription to /api/notify/subscribe.
Unsupported browser OR denied permission shows a clear message, including the iOS 'install to Home Screen first' caveat (ADR-008).
Event opt-in toggles (incl. 'stage') + quiet-hours start/end inputs, loaded from and saved to /api/notify/preferences.
Loading, error, and permission/empty states handled; /settings link added to Nav.tsx.
tasks.action: use client. Follow src/app/tasks/page.tsx structure + tokens (var(--bg), var(--surface), var(--border), var(--text), var(--accent), --sp-3, --radius, var(--font-sans) from globals.css). Enable flow inside a click handler (user gesture), guard serviceWorker/PushManager support. urlBase64ToUint8Array helper inline for applicationServerKey. NEXT_PUBLIC_VAPID_PUBLIC_KEY (public key, bundle-safe). Add { href: '/settings', label: 'Settings' } to the LINKS array in Nav.tsx.
